health.mental_health: High stress and persistent worry related to her teenager’s escalating school issues; intermittent insomnia and irritability, but she is trying coping strategies and considering short-term counseling.
recent_visits: Ophthalmology follow-up for intraocular pressure check (last month)
Primary care visit to review kidney labs and blood pressure (6 weeks ago)
Pulmonology check-in and spirometry for COPD maintenance (3 months ago)
Urgent care visit for shortness of breath during a cold (early winter)

finance.income_range: $55,000–$75,000/year (variable with flight hours and per diem)
finance.debt: Small remaining balance on a condo mortgage; recent increase in out-of-pocket costs for her teenager’s tutoring, school-related fees, and a consultation with a behavioral health specialist.
recent_transactions: Paid for private math tutoring sessions for her teenager
Co-pay for ophthalmology visit and glaucoma drops refill
Purchased an air purifier and replacement filters for home (COPD-related)
Paid an attorney consultation fee about school discipline/IEP options
Monthly payment to a condo association (HOA dues)

relationships: Jay Park (adult son in Seattle; supportive but worried about the household)
Mina Park (teenage granddaughter living with Lauren; ongoing conflicts over school and rules)
Eun-hee Park (Lauren’s sister in Wasilla; helps with rides and occasional respite)
Nina Caldwell (neighbor in Anchorage; checks in during layovers)
A small circle of flight attendant friends based in Anchorage who share trip swaps
social.social_media_activity: Uses Facebook mostly to follow family and Anchorage community updates; posts occasional photos of sunsets and travel views but avoids sharing details about home life. Keeps her profile mostly private and rarely tags locations in real time.
private_conversations: Discussions with the school counselor about suspensions, vaping rumors, and skipping classes
Text exchanges with her sister about whether Mina may need a formal evaluation (IEP/504) and therapy
Conversations with her son about guardianship boundaries and whether Mina should temporarily live elsewhere
Calls with a close coworker about feeling unsafe leaving Mina home alone during multi-day trips

schedule.work_hours: Irregular; typically 12–16 days/month with early check-ins and 2–4 day trips. She bids for shorter Anchorage-based routes when possible due to home responsibilities.
regular_appointments: Quarterly lab work for kidney function (eGFR/creatinine) via primary care
Weekly check-in call with school counselor (during the school term)
upcoming_events: School meeting with vice principal and counselor to review behavior plan (next week)
Pulmonary rehab orientation session (in two weeks)
Union scheduling/bidding deadline for next month’s roster (end of month)
Eye clinic appointment for visual field test (next month)
schedule.daily_routine: On non-flying days she wakes early, uses inhaler, checks her blood pressure, and handles school emails and attendance calls. Afternoons are split between errands, meal prep, and monitoring homework; evenings are structured around curfew checks and trying to de-escalate arguments before bed.

professional.company: A major U.S. airline with an Anchorage crew base (Alaska Airlines mainline)
projects: Recurrent annual safety and emergency procedures training
Volunteering as a peer mentor for new-hire cabin crew on Anchorage routes
Working with a union rep to adjust bidding strategy for more predictable pairings
professional.performance_issues: Has requested last-minute trip drops twice in the past two months due to urgent school calls and a meeting related to her teenager’s discipline; worries this pattern could affect her standing and is trying to formalize a more stable schedule.
workplace_relationships: Good rapport with inflight supervisors but feels pressure to maintain reliability
A trusted coworker (Denise H.) who occasionally swaps trips to help her cover school meetings
Union representative contact for bidding accommodations and leave options

hobbies: Walking the Coastal Trail when weather allows
Knitting and mending uniforms and winter gear
Cooking Korean comfort food and freezing portions for trip weeks
habits: Uses a pill organizer and sets phone alarms for eye drops and inhaler timing
Keeps a home peak-flow meter and tracks breathing on a notepad
Avoids NSAIDs due to kidney disease and sticks to acetaminophen for pain
Occasionally drinks a nightcap to fall asleep when household conflict escalates
Keeps a locked cabinet for medications and valuables because of concerns about her teenager’s impulsive behavior
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives in a two-bedroom condo in Anchorage and is the primary guardian in practice for her teenage granddaughter, who has been having escalating behavioral issues and school problems; Lauren tries to balance household stability with frequent work travel by leaning on her sister and a neighbor for backup.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Low-sodium, kidney- and blood-pressure-conscious meals; limits processed foods, focuses on fish, soups, rice, and vegetables, and keeps caffeine modest to avoid worsening insomnia.
